title: Nasa weberbaueri
intro: Nasa weberbaueri, also known as "Peruvian tea", is a plant species native to the Andean region of Peru, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The leaves of Nasa weberbaueri have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Nasa weberbaueri is commonly used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into supplements and traditional medicine formulations.
taxonomy: Nasa weberbaueri is a species of flowering plant in the family Loasaceae. It belongs to the genus Nasa, which consists of about 50 species of flowering plants. Nasa weberbaueri is native to the Andes mountain range in Peru. The classification of Nasa weberbaueri is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Cornales, Family: Loasaceae, Genus: Nasa, Species: N. weberbaueri.
